plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Trim SMH and SPY to reduce concentration.
Increase allocation to income ETF QQQI.
Add new positions in XLV and TXN for diversification.
Hold a larger cash buffer for safety.
Maintain tight stops on all positions.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of ~1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns.

moves.note: Add quality tech with good scan

plan: Trim leveraged ETF SOXL to a small allocation.
Reduce concentration in SPY and SMH.
Increase QQQ allocation for broader tech exposure.
Add new positions in LRCX and GOOGL.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
Balance growth with risk reduction.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets ~2.5% monthly growth by blending broad indices, quality tech, and a small amount of leverage. It aims for risk-first growth with controlled drawdowns.

moves.note: Add quality tech with good scan

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged SOXL.
Trim SPY to meet concentration limits.
Increase SMH and CRWD exposure.
Add new positions in ASML and GEV.
Maintain a small cash buffer.
Utilize wider but strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into leverage and concentrated high-scoring tech names. It accepts significantly higher potential drawdowns and the risk of leveraged ETF decay.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta tech.
Maximize allocation to income ETF QQQI.
Increase SPY for stability and income potential.
Add defensive sector ETF XLV.
Hold a significant cash buffer.
Prioritize steady monthly distributions over growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets ~1% monthly distributions by prioritizing income ETFs and broad indices. It minimizes volatility and drawdowns, parking cash for yield, not growth.
